data.description:

Theglobe.com, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company, based in Dallas, says it cannot complete the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on time due to unanticipated delays, without giving further detail.

Management states that the 10-K (Yearly Report) will be filed within the allowed 15-day extension window under SEC rules. Theglobe.com also confirms it is current on all other required SEC filings over the past 12 months, signaling no broader compliance backlog.

The company does not expect any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, based on information available at this time. As always, any expectations about timing or financial outcomes should be viewed as forward-looking and subject to change.

The notification is signed by Frederick Jones, who serves as Chief Executive Officer, Chief Financial Officer and sole board member. His dual role highlights a very lean governance structure as the company works to complete its annual reporting obligations and maintain SEC compliance.

data.title: Landmark Bancorp Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Landmark Bancorp ( (LARK) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Landmark Bancorp, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company’s 2025 Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is being filed late, even though all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been submitted on time.

The delay stems from an internal investigation into alleged misconduct by a non-executive employee at Landmark National Bank, launched after the January 28, 2026 earnings release. Management and the Audit and Risk Committee, supported by outside counsel and advisors, are reviewing the matter and its implications for internal controls and the annual closing process.

Landmark Bancorp expects to file the 2025 Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-day extension window allowed under SEC rules. Management emphasizes that it is working under the oversight of the Audit and Risk Committee to complete all additional procedures as soon as reasonably possible.

The company currently does not expect the amounts at issue to materially impact previously reported periods or its January 28, 2026 earnings release, and it does not anticipate significant changes in operating results versus the prior year. However, it cautions that the investigation is ongoing, actual results may differ from current expectations, and all timing and outcome statements are forward-looking and subject to risk.

Landmark Bancorp states it is assessing the effectiveness of its internal control over financial reporting and aims to remain in full SEC compliance once the 2025 Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed. The notification is signed on April 1, 2026 by Mark A. Herpich, Vice President, Secretary, Treasurer and Chief Financial Officer, underscoring executive accountability for the disclosure.

The most recent analyst rating on (LARK) stock is a Buy with a $31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Landmark Bancorp stock, see the LARK Stock Forecast page.

Technicals are supportive with price above key moving averages and positive momentum indicators. Valuation adds a boost due to the low P/E and ~3% dividend yield, while the latest earnings call was broadly positive but included cost pressures and investment-portfolio unrealized losses.

To see Spark’s full report on LARK stock, click here.

More about Landmark Bancorp

Landmark Bancorp, Inc. is a bank holding company headquartered in Manhattan, Kansas, and operates through its wholly owned subsidiary, Landmark National Bank. The group focuses on community banking services, including lending and deposit products, primarily in regional markets across Kansas.

data.title: Rimini Street Amends Credit Agreement, Updates Capital Return Limits
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rimini Street ( (RMNI) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, Rimini Street, Inc. amended its Amended and Restated Credit Agreement with its lending group led by Capital One to revise the limits on permitted “Restricted Payments,” including share repurchases and similar equity-related payouts. Beginning with the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, Restricted Payments are capped at $20 million per year, and, for payments made on and after January 1, 2026, total Restricted Payments may not exceed $50 million, subject to existing conditions in the credit facility.

Previously, annual Restricted Payments through the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, were limited to the greater of $12.5 million and 20% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, and aggregate Restricted Payments over the life of the facility were subject to a higher ceiling tied to up to 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA. The amendment refines Rimini Street’s capital return flexibility within its borrowing covenants, clarifying the scope and scale of potential stock repurchases and other equity-related cash uses while maintaining the agreed risk allocation between the company and its lenders.

The most recent analyst rating on (RMNI) stock is a Hold with a $3.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rimini Street stock, see the RMNI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RMNI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RMNI is a Neutral.

The Middleby ( (MIDD) ) just unveiled an update.

On April 1, 2026, The Middleby Corporation announced that Amy Campbell will serve as Chief Financial Officer of its Food Processing business once it is spun off as an independent public company in the second quarter of 2026. The appointment, effective immediately, follows the earlier naming of Mark Salman as CEO and completes the executive leadership team that will guide the business after separation.

Campbell brings extensive experience from prior CFO roles at REV Group, ASC Engineered Solutions, and BrandSafway’s Commercial and Industrial Division, along with a long tenure at Caterpillar in senior finance and investor relations positions. Middleby said the finalized leadership team, combined with the planned spin-off, supports its broader portfolio transformation strategy to create three focused businesses and is expected to give the Food Processing unit greater flexibility in capital allocation and growth through strategic investments and acquisitions.

data.title: FitLife Brands Q4 2025 Results Highlight Irwin Acquisition
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FitLife Brands ( (FTLF) ) just unveiled an update.

FitLife Brands reported its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results on April 1, 2026, showing that the August 8 acquisition of Irwin Naturals drove a 73% year-on-year jump in quarterly revenue to $25.9 million and a 26% rise in annual revenue to $81.5 million. However, Irwin’s lower-margin profile and related transaction and inventory amortization costs weighed on profitability, with gross margins narrowing, quarterly net income slipping to $1.6 million, full-year net income falling to $6.3 million, and earnings per share declining even as adjusted EBITDA held roughly flat and leverage ended the year at about $43.1 million in net debt.

In the quarter, wholesale revenue more than tripled to $15.5 million, fueled by $11.2 million in Irwin sales, while online revenue grew modestly to $10.5 million and Irwin products ramped on Amazon from zero to roughly $0.5 million in December. For the full year, wholesale revenue surged 84% to $39.7 million as online sales dipped 3% to $41.8 million, highlighting a shift in channel mix and strengthening wholesale presence even as legacy FitLife wholesale sales declined in the fourth quarter and the company absorbed higher financing obligations with $39.1 million outstanding on its term loan and $5.6 million on its revolver at year-end 2025.

The most recent analyst rating on (FTLF) stock is a Hold with a $15.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FitLife Brands stock, see the FTLF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FTLF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FTLF is a Neutral.

Overall score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (growth and improving leverage), tempered by meaningful margin compression and softer 2025 cash generation. Technicals remain weak (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while valuation is only middling at ~24x earnings with no dividend support. Earnings call commentary adds a mixed backdrop: acquisition-driven growth and brand wins offset by margin pressure and signs of consumer softness.

To see Spark’s full report on FTLF stock, click here.

More about FitLife Brands

FitLife Brands, Inc., based in Omaha and listed on Nasdaq as FTLF, provides innovative and proprietary nutritional supplements and wellness products. The company sells through online channels and wholesale distribution, and in 2025 expanded its portfolio and scale with the acquisition of Irwin Naturals, which historically operates at lower gross margins than FitLife’s legacy brands.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Completes Landmark JDE Peet’s Acquisition
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Keurig Dr Pepper ( (KDP) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, Keurig Dr Pepper issued $4.5 billion of Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock and received a $4 billion capital contribution from a new pod manufacturing joint venture, using the proceeds to help finance its cash acquisition of JDE Peet’s and simultaneously terminating a previously arranged 364-day bridge credit facility. The joint venture partner, managed by Apollo, KKR and Goldman Sachs Asset Management, acquired a 49% stake in Keurig JV, LP, which now holds KDP’s U.S. and Canadian K-Cup pod and single-serve manufacturing assets, reinforcing KDP’s coffee infrastructure and funding while sharing ownership of key production capabilities.

Following a merger protocol signed in August 2025, KDP’s subsidiary Kodiak BidCo declared its offer for JDE Peet’s unconditional on March 27, 2026, and on April 1, 2026, settled the offer at €31.85 per share in cash for 466,712,270 tendered shares, representing 96.22% of JDE Peet’s and about €14.86 billion in consideration. The deal, financed through debt offerings, the JV sale, preferred stock issuance and a term loan, positions KDP to combine its Keurig system with JDE Peet’s global brands, delist JDE Peet’s from Euronext Amsterdam by April 30, 2026, and, after integration, execute a planned separation into two U.S.-listed companies, with Rafael Oliveira leading the future Global Coffee Co. and Tim Cofer heading Beverage Co.

The most recent analyst rating on (KDP) stock is a Buy with a $38.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Keurig Dr Pepper stock, see the KDP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KDP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KDP is a Neutral.

KDP scores as a moderately attractive but mixed setup: solid financial performance and a reasonable valuation/dividend are partly offset by weak technical momentum and near-term earnings pressure from coffee inflation, brewer declines, and transaction/JV-related costs, alongside elevated leverage tied to the acquisition and separation plan.

To see Spark’s full report on KDP stock, click here.

More about Keurig Dr Pepper

Keurig Dr Pepper is a leading North American beverage company with more than 125 owned, licensed and partner brands across carbonated soft drinks, coffee, tea, water, juice and mixers, and it operates the #1 single-serve coffee brewing system in the U.S. and Canada. With over $16 billion in annual revenue and 30,000 employees, it targets broad beverage occasions while building emerging platforms in premium coffee, energy, sports hydration and ready-to-drink coffee.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Greenlane Holdings ( (GNLN) ) just unveiled an update.

Greenlane reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results on March 31, 2026, highlighting a completed strategic transition away from legacy wholesale and distribution operations to a Berachain-focused digital asset treasury model. Following an October 2025 private placement that raised $110.7 million, the company accumulated 51.7 million BERA tokens by year-end at a $58.3 million cost basis, alongside $22.6 million in stablecoins and no debt, but recognized a $31.1 million fair value loss on BERA and sharply higher operating and net losses tied to non-cash stock-based compensation, inventory impairments, and restructuring.

In Q4 2025, Greenlane’s revenue fell 18% year on year to $1.4 million and net loss widened to $69.6 million, while full-year 2025 revenue dropped 67% to $4.4 million with net loss deepening to $85.6 million as legacy operations wound down. Subsequent events included a March 25, 2026 Nasdaq delisting notice over minimum bid price non-compliance, shareholder approval that same day for a reverse stock split at a 1-for-5 to 1-for-15 ratio, and February 2026 token purchase and lending agreements designed to expand BERA exposure and liquidity, underscoring both the company’s aggressive pivot into the Berachain ecosystem and the listing and valuation risks facing equity holders.

data.title: Eversource Challenges FERC Ruling on Transmission ROE
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Eversource Energy ( (ES) ) has provided an update.

On March 19, 2026, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission set a base return on equity of 9.57% and a maximum incentive ROE of 12.09% for New England transmission owners, prompting Eversource Energy to challenge the order as inconsistent with the Federal Power Act and based on outdated data. Eversource warned that the ruling, including potential retroactive refunds beyond the usual statutory limit, could undermine investor confidence, raise capital costs, slow critical transmission expansion and increase long‑term customer costs, and said the lower allowed ROE is expected to cut its 2026 after‑tax earnings by about $70 million and has led it to trim its 2026 non‑GAAP earnings guidance to $4.57–$4.72 per share, while still targeting 5–7% annual EPS growth through 2030 from a $4.65 base.

Eversource and other New England transmission owners are preparing legal and regulatory responses, including a request for rehearing, a motion for stay and a potential Section 205 rate filing, while any required customer refunds tied to prior periods would be spread over 18 to 24 months if the order is upheld. The company argues that FERC’s long period of inaction and the new ROE framework heighten regulatory risk, increase the likelihood of credit downgrades and could make future transmission projects more expensive, ultimately threatening regional reliability, affordability and economic growth despite past transmission investments that have delivered significant congestion‑related savings for customers.

The most recent analyst rating on (ES) stock is a Hold with a $73.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Eversource Energy stock, see the ES Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ES Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ES is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weaker cash flow conversion and elevated leverage despite improving profitability. Technicals are supportive with strong price momentum, and valuation is reasonable with a solid ~4% dividend yield. Management’s guidance is constructive, but sizable financing needs and regulatory/timing uncertainties temper the outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on ES stock, click here.

More about Eversource Energy

Eversource Energy, listed on the NYSE as ES, is a major New England utility and transmission owner focused on electric transmission infrastructure and related services in the region. The company invests heavily in expanding and modernizing the regional grid to support reliability, integrate additional energy supply and address energy affordability for customers across New England.

data.title: Alexander’s Amends Bloomberg Lease and Loan Reserve Structure
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alexander’s ( (ALX) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 31, 2026, Alexander’s subsidiary 731 Office One LLC amended Bloomberg L.P.’s lease at 731 Lexington Avenue, granting a rent abatement of $56.8 million for the period from April 1, 2026 to December 1, 2026 while correspondingly cutting Bloomberg’s tenant improvement fund from $113.6 million to $56.8 million. The core lease terms, including Bloomberg’s full occupancy of the office condominium and the February 8, 2040 expiration date, remain unchanged.

Also on March 31, 2026, Alexander’s amended its September 30, 2024 loan agreement to establish a free rent reserve account of $56.8 million, largely funded from an existing lender reserve, so that during the abatement period Bloomberg’s forgone rent is effectively redirected to support debt service. Monthly draws from this reserve will cover Bloomberg’s base rent obligations for loan payments, with any surplus distributed to Alexander’s, mitigating cash flow disruption from the temporary rent relief.

An announcement from Sow Good ( (SOWG) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Sow Good raised $3 million through the private sale of 1.5 million shares of Series AAA Convertible Redeemable Preferred Stock to a non-U.S. investor group led through a Panamanian holding company, with each preferred share initially convertible into 250 common shares and ranking senior to common stock in a liquidation. The financing, exempt from U.S. registration requirements and earmarked for general corporate purposes and working capital, introduces a highly dilutive security with limited voting rights but strong economic and redemption preferences, potentially reshaping the company’s capital structure and control dynamics.

Also on March 31, 2026, the company underwent a major leadership and board overhaul, with CEO David Lazar and CFO Donna Guy resigning from their officer roles, five directors stepping down, and Chief Commercial Officer Yisroel Goldberg being elevated to both chief executive and financial officer. Goldberg, along with newly appointed directors Binyomin Posen, Joseph Labkowski and Jack Wortzman, now constitute a reconstituted board and committee leadership that signals a strategic reset for Sow Good’s governance and oversight, even as the departing executives and directors cited no disagreements over policies or operations.

data.title: SkyWater Sets Shareholder Vote on IonQ Cash-and-Stock Takeover
data.description:
New activity is brewing for SkyWater Technology ( (SKYT) ). The company has submitted a Form DEFM14A to the SEC, indicating an upcoming shareholder vote. This form gives shareholders all the information they need to develop an informed vote. Read on to learn more about the proposed company action.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SkyWater Technology has agreed to be acquired by IonQ in a two-step merger that will ultimately make the chipmaker a wholly owned subsidiary of the quantum computing company. Under the terms, SkyWater shareholders are set to receive $15 in cash plus IonQ stock valued via a formula tied to IonQ’s 20-day volume-weighted average price, with the exchange ratio capped and floored to limit dilution and pricing volatility.

Completion of the transaction hinges on approval from a majority of SkyWater’s outstanding shares at a special virtual shareholder meeting scheduled for May 8, 2026. The SkyWater board has unanimously deemed the merger fair and in the best interests of investors, strongly urging shareholders to vote in favor, a stance that underscores the strategic importance of combining a specialty semiconductor foundry with a fast-growing quantum computing firm.

The most recent analyst rating on (SKYT) stock is a Hold with a $28.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on SkyWater Technology stock, see the SKYT Stock Forecast page.

Technical indicators are currently soft, while valuation appears attractive on a low P/E and corporate events are supportive due to the pending IonQ acquisition.

To see Spark’s full report on SKYT stock, click here.

More about SkyWater Technology

SkyWater Technology, Inc. is a U.S.-based semiconductor manufacturer whose common stock trades on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol SKYT. Main Street Capital ( (MAIN) ) has shared an update.

On March 27, 2026, Main Street Capital Corporation entered into an underwriting agreement with RBC Capital Markets to issue an additional $200 million of 6.95% notes due 2029, which were sold in a public offering that closed on March 31, 2026. These new notes form a single, fungible series with the $350 million of 6.95% notes issued in January 2024, bringing the total outstanding principal of this 2029 debt to $550 million and maintaining equal ranking with Main Street’s other unsecured obligations while remaining structurally subordinated to subsidiary-level debt.

The notes pay 6.95% interest semiannually, mature on March 1, 2029, and include standard redemption and change-of-control repurchase features, as well as covenants tied to Investment Company Act asset coverage and ongoing financial disclosure. Main Street received approximately $202.8 million in net proceeds and plans to initially use the capital to repay outstanding borrowings under its credit facilities, which should reduce near-term funding costs and give the lender additional balance sheet flexibility for future investments.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

International Seaways ( (INSW) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of International Seaways entered into a joinder agreement to its existing $500 million revolving credit facility, becoming a subsidiary guarantor under that financing. As part of the agreement, the subsidiary pledged a VLCC tanker it owns as collateral, designating the vessel as a substitution asset replacing ships previously sold or released from the secured collateral pool, thereby maintaining the strength and continuity of the company’s borrowing base.

The move reflects International Seaways’ ongoing active management of its secured credit structure as it refreshes collateral in line with fleet changes. By substituting a modern VLCC into the facility’s collateral package, the company helps preserve access to liquidity under the revolving credit facility and supports financial flexibility for its tanker operations and capital allocation plans.
